text: ‘No second chance’; literally, ‘no room for repentance’. This seems to have been an idiom of Roman law, meaning an opportunity to reconsider one’s decision; in this case, the decision of Esau to sell his birthright.
Cf. Deut. 9.19; where, however, a different occasion is referred to.
‘In gratitude for this, let us worship’; this may also mean, ‘We have grace given us, to worship’.
